Tiago explains the precise strategy the French community uses

“The precise strategy is really looking for the volume. You look for the volume. I mean, they use a tool, I think it's called, Fabriq. And then they enter, but they don't do 90%, so they go for spot. So when they mean full range is 70 bins maximum and they wait and then the volume is there, it has to be a 5% or 10% pool And then they wait for five minutes maximum and they exit with I don't know when are 4 or 5% in profit.”

“It's always like I said they’re surgeons they always going to the bubble map. The holder count looking for the narrative. For example if it's a KOL led coin, like it's a red flag, don't go for it. ”

Anchor 1.0 just shipped, and it's a big one for @solana_devs. Here's what each change means for your workflow:

- No Solana CLI dependency → one fewer tool to install, no more version conflicts

- Surfpool + LiteSVM defaults → Surfpool replaces the local validator for testing, LiteSVM is the new default test template. Faster across the board

- Migration<From, To> → upgrade account schemas on live programs without manual deserialization

- Lifecycle hooks → run linters, security checks, or custom scripts pre/post build, test, and deploy from Anchor.toml

- avm self-update → update AVM without reinstalling through Cargo

- declare_program! parser → decode raw instruction data from the IDL automatically

- Duplicate mutable accounts rejected → prevents a whole class of exploits by default, opt in with dup if you need it

🚨 Drift Protocol Hack — Key Update (Apr 3, 2026)
One of the largest DeFi exploits this year has hit Solana.

▫️~$285M drained (est. $270–286M)
▫️ >50% TVL wiped in ~12 minutes
▫️ Assets stolen: USDC, SOL, JLP, WBTC & more
▫️ 2nd largest hack in Solana history (after Wormhole)

 ⚠️ Attack vector (not a code exploit):
The attacker leveraged durable nonces — a legitimate Solana feature — to execute pre-signed admin transactions prepared weeks in advance.
→ Gained control of the Security Council (multisig)
→ Removed withdrawal limits
→ Drained protocol vaults at high speed

👉 Post-exploit flow:
▫️ Funds bridged to Ethereum via Circle’s CCTP
▫️~129K ETH moved cross-chain

🔎 Notable developments:
▫️ Suspected DPRK/Lazarus Group involvement (pattern-based attribution)
▫️Criticism toward Circle for delayed USDC freeze (~6h)
▫️Contagion impact: 10+ Solana protocols affected
▫️ $DRIFT token dropped ~30–37% at lows

📍 Current status:
 ▫️Investigation ongoing
 ▫️ No confirmed fund recovery yet
 ▫️Solana core infra unaffected — but DeFi trust takes a hit.

🧩 This wasn’t a smart contract bug — it was a governance + key management failure.
Durable nonces + compromised admin flow = catastrophic risk.

⚠️Stay cautious. Monitor trusted sources like Arkham, PeckShield, Elliptic, and @DriftProtocol for updates.

This week is a reminder that security hygiene isn't optional.

We wrote up everything you need to know about protecting your RPC tokens and endpoints: backend vs frontend auth, how tokens get leaked, and how to make sure yours don't.

The short version:
- You have two ways to authenticate your RPC endpoint: secret tokens (backend) and allowed origins (frontend)
- If your token gets leaked, anyone can route traffic through your endpoint (leading to unexpected charges)
- Treat your secret token like a private key: never log it, commit it (even to private repos), or put it in frontend code

Drift was hit by a very sophisticated attack, the source of which is still being investigated

There was no program or smart contract exploit 
 
Like almost all crypto hacks of late (Bybit, Neutrl, Step), this ultimately appears to be opsec/social engineering vs contract risk

The unfortunate reality is that this happened to a Solana-based entity, but it could have happened to any protocol protected by a multisig on any chain

There’s likely to be a lot of lessons to be learned and socialized once the full investigation is complete, but this is an isolated incident and says nothing about Solana DeFi or any other Solana product

Of course, my heart goes out to everyone affected by this

Today was very hard on many people including lots of friends and I’m personally filled with tremendous sadness, because nobody deserves this

But I know the Solana DeFi community will pick up the pieces and rebuild fast as only Solana can

Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Drift Protocol through a novel attack involving durable nonces, resulting in a rapid takeover of Drift’s Security Council administrative powers.

the strat is spot -10%, single side sol. the exit rule is strict: when price touches about half of my position and starts going out of range.

the whole edge is in executing the exit exactly when the condition hits, not when it feels right.
